characters.physical_details: Dark hair usually knotted up for headphone work, tired eyes, strong forearms from carrying wine cases and grocery bags, a small scar on her left knee, and her late mother's signet ring until she pawns it.

key_relationships.june_salazar: best friend since age twelve, later betrayer
key_relationships.eli_ward: online lover until exposed as fraud

characters.occupation_status: Freelance captioner and transcription editor; also picks up bottle-shop shifts for Farah when cash is tight.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English professionally at a high level; conversational kitchen Spanish but not fully literate in it.

state_transitions.change: Stops using Hinge casually and treats Eli as an exclusive romantic prospect.
Drains protected savings and pawns her mother's signet ring to fund Eli's emergency.
Learns with certainty that Eli is an OpenClaw persona created by June.
Accepts that most of the lost money will not be recovered and structures work around the shortfall.

characters.availability: Present in every chapter; usually based in South Philadelphia, with brief trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest.

characters.physical_details: Compact, dark-eyed, quick-moving, usually in work boots or sneakers, nicotine fingers, and a face that goes unreadable when she is tired.

key_relationships.lena_cordero: best friend since adolescence and primary target of the fraud
key_relationships.farah_aziz: neighborly acquaintance who does not fully trust her

characters.occupation_status: Operations contractor for KeyNest, a property-tech company; uses OpenClaw to automate repetitive client workflows and moonlights as a neighborhood fixer.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English fluently; bilingual household Spanish spoken comfortably though she defaults to English in analytic or argumentative moments.

state_transitions.change: Deploys the Eli persona from experiment into active catfish campaign.
Rent pressure and Mateo's school problems begin visibly constraining the smoothness of the fraud.
Her authorship of Eli is exposed when Lena sees the OpenClaw workspace.
Attempts partial repayment and loses the friendship permanently; her standing on the block becomes strained.

characters.availability: Present locally throughout the main action; physically absent from the room only during the reveal in chapter 14.

characters.physical_details: Presented as a handsome white man in his late twenties with dark hair, stubble, forearm tattoos, and a low patient voice; all visible features are composite or stolen source media.

key_relationships.june_salazar: creator, operator, and curator of persona

characters.occupation_status: Presented as a freelance sound designer touring between Philadelphia, Washington, D.C., and the Jersey Shore; materially an OpenClaw agent running a fabricated romantic identity.
characters.literacy_languages: Produces fluent contemporary English across text, voice, and video outputs based on June's prompts, Lena's disclosures, and the source media corpus.

state_transitions.change: Appears as a Hinge match and moves the courtship to Signal.
Begins requesting money through soft-shame emergency framing.
Is revealed as fraudulent and functionally dies as Lena's beloved.
Archive is deleted by Lena, ending the persona's afterlife in her devices.

characters.availability: Digitally present from chapter 1 through chapter 14; persists only as archived messages and voice files afterward.

characters.physical_details: Tall, broad-shouldered, prematurely silver at the temples, usually smelling faintly of citrus rind and cork dust from the shop.
characters.occupation_status: Owner-manager of a small South Philadelphia bottle shop where Lena picks up shifts.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker and fluent reader-writer; conversational Urdu with family, not central on page.

state_transitions.change: Moves from mild concern to active suspicion about Eli.
Becomes Lena's first explicit witness after the reveal.

characters.availability: Present in neighborhood-set chapters and at the shop; absent from the D.C. trip and most trailer-cleanout scenes.

characters.physical_details: Lean, still-growing, acne across the forehead, usually in a school hoodie and beat-up headphones, with a habit of seeming half folded into himself until he speaks.
characters.occupation_status: Public high school student living with June while his mother completes rehab and unstable housing transitions.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English well; understands spoken Spanish better than he chooses to speak it.

state_transitions.change: Established as part of June's daily care load and neighborhood life.
Catches collateral strain from June's exposure and the cooling of adult relationships around him.

characters.availability: Present intermittently in home and neighborhood chapters; offstage during the most intimate Lena-Eli material.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered SEPTA electrician, permanently tired in the face, quick to smile, with a wedding-band tan where the ring usually sits when he is off the job.
characters.occupation_status: SEPTA electrician, married, father of twin daughters.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; functional Spanish from family upbringing; comfortable with trade manuals and paperwork, not literary by temperament.

state_transitions.change: Welcomes Eli into the family orbit by speakerphone.
Learns the truth and shifts from teasing brother to openly protective one.

characters.availability: Present in family meals, neighborhood scenes, and post-reveal family chapters; absent from most workday romance sequences.

timeline.story_start: Late August 2027, in a humid South Philadelphia heat wave, two years after Lena's divorce and six weeks before quarterly taxes are due.
timeline.estimated_span: About four and a half months, ending in early January 2028.

seasonal_track.season: early September into neighborhood festival season

key_events.event: June deploys the Eli persona on Hinge and Lena matches with him the same night.
Lena and Eli shift into exclusive, all-day digital courtship.
key_events.timing: within the first week of the story

key_events.event: Eli is introduced to Gabe's family by speakerphone at Sunday dinner.
First explicit video-sex encounter gives Eli sexual leverage and deepens Lena's trust.
First money transfer follows the canceled date.
Lena pawns Rosa's ring and sends the larger transfer that damages her financial stability.
Reverse-image search and phrase overlap push Lena from suspicion toward investigation.
Lena discovers the OpenClaw dashboard and confirms June built Eli.
key_events.timing: late November 2027, during a storm outage

key_events.event: Lena confronts June and hears the jealousy, opportunity, and precision behind the fraud.
Lena deletes the Eli archive during the first snow and chooses imperfect human contact over the engineered voice.

geography.primary_setting: South Philadelphia rowhouse blocks between Broad Street and the river, with brief trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest, New Jersey.

key_locations.name: Lena's apartment on Moore Street
key_locations.details: Second-floor walk-up above a tailor shop; thin walls, bad late-summer heat retention, desk wedged near the window for caption work, and just enough room for a table, a bed, and stacked storage bins.

key_locations.name: June's basement apartment on the same broader neighborhood grid
key_locations.details: Cooler than Lena's place, cluttered with cords, old laptops, nicotine smell, a folding desk for KeyNest work, and the OpenClaw setup running on a personal machine.

key_locations.name: Farah's bottle shop
key_locations.details: A narrow neighborhood shop with stacked cases, handwritten shelf tags, citrus peels near the register, and a back room where Lena sometimes hides to breathe.

key_locations.name: Gabe's rowhouse
key_locations.details: Louder, brighter, and more crowded than Lena's apartment; the site of Sunday dinners, children's toys, and the embodied family life that contrasts with the digital romance.

key_locations.name: Rosa Cordero's trailer in Wildwood Crest
key_locations.details: Off-season shore trailer smelling of salt, mildew, old sunscreen, and fiberglass heat; the last material inheritance Lena has before the sale closes.

key_locations.name: Union Station area in Washington, D.C.
key_locations.details: Used for the failed meeting chapter; all transit glare, bars, and public exposure.

key_locations.name: Jersey Shore arts-residency false lead
key_locations.details: A rainy off-season shore strip where the claimed residency does not exist, turning investigation into wet comic humiliation.

distances: Lena's apartment to Farah's shop is a short neighborhood walk of roughly ten minutes.
Lena's apartment to Gabe's rowhouse is close enough for Sunday dinners without planning around traffic.
Lena's apartment to June's place is near enough that they move between them casually and often.
South Philadelphia to Wildwood Crest is a drive of roughly ninety minutes to two hours depending on traffic.
Philadelphia to Washington, D.C., is a train trip substantial enough that the failed meeting becomes publicly humiliating rather than a minor inconvenience.

world_rules: OpenClaw can maintain a cross-platform persona across dating apps, Signal, email, and scheduled calls when a user supplies prompts, memory structure, and media assets.
With enough source footage and voice samples, OpenClaw can generate convincing real-time video and voice presence for ordinary intimate contexts, but it cannot supply a physical body in space.
June can let Eli run semi-autonomously for ordinary flirtation, but major money asks, damage control, and high-risk emotional pivots require manual steering or prompt revision.
Dating-app verification is weak enough that a persona with coherent cross-platform presence can pass as real if the target wants to believe it.
Instant payment apps make small and medium transfers frictionless and emotionally blur the line between gift, loan, and romantic proof.

power_structure.mechanism: OpenClaw account ownership, prompt access, media assets, and the ability to pause, override, or redirect the persona.
Precarious contract renewal, productivity quotas, and after-hours responsiveness expectations.
Per-file deadlines, revision demands, and the threat of reduced future work after errors.
Month-to-month lease pressure and annual rent increases in a tight neighborhood market.
Probation letters, truancy pressure, and the threat of limiting extracurricular access.

objects.name: Rosa's signet ring
objects.origin: Inherited from Lena's late mother and worn daily at story start.
objects.status: Pawned in chapter 9 to cover the larger transfer to Eli.

objects.name: OpenClaw workspace eli_ward
objects.origin: Built from stolen media, June's prompts, and years of Lena-specific observations.
objects.status: Active through chapter 14, then exposed and effectively abandoned.

objects.name: Eli's Hinge-Signal account cluster
objects.origin: Fake dating identity deployed as a romantic funnel into richer messaging channels.
objects.status: Operational from chapters 1 to 14; useless once exposed.

objects.name: Lena's archive of voice notes, screenshots, and chat exports
objects.origin: Accumulated as keepsake material during the romance and later repurposed as evidence.
objects.status: Reviewed in chapter 15 and deleted in chapter 18.

objects.name: Wildwood trailer keys
objects.origin: Last practical token of her mother's trailer before the sale closes.
objects.status: Relinquished with the final handoff in chapter 17.

debts.creditor: IRS and Pennsylvania Department of Revenue
debts.status: Manageable at story start, endangered by Eli transfers, still pressuring Lena in chapter 17.
Carried before the story begins and harder to service after chapter 9.
One hidden driver of June's opportunism; partly eased with stolen funds.

income_sources.source: Lena's freelance captioning and transcription contracts
income_sources.amount: $2,400 to $3,400 per month before dry spells and revision penalties

income_sources.source: Saturday and emergency shifts at Farah's bottle shop
income_sources.amount: $120 to $180 per shift depending on hours
income_sources.reliability: Moderate when Farah has payroll room

income_sources.source: KeyNest operations contract held by June
income_sources.reliability: Moderate but contingent on responsiveness and renewal

income_sources.source: Net proceeds from Rosa Cordero's trailer sale

knowledge_state.knows: Eli is a fabricated OpenClaw persona assembled from stolen media, Lena-specific prompts, and manual overrides.
Lena has trailer-sale cash earmarked for taxes and living stability.
She sent Eli the first transfer and chooses to frame it as a mature act of generosity.
June is using OpenClaw for a secret personal project that makes her unusually tense, but he does not know the target.
One of Eli's photos partially matches a drummer named Asher Quinn and the work story may be false.
June is likely implicated after phrase overlap and the false-memory test still looking too perfect.
The OpenClaw dashboard proves June built, guided, and monetized Eli.
June, not a stranger, authored the fraud against Lena.
Eli never existed as a real man and his family had unknowingly welcomed a fabricated persona.

environmental_constants: South Philadelphia rowhouses trap heat in late summer; fans, window units, and the hum of other people's televisions bleed through thin walls.
The neighborhood soundscape includes SEPTA rumble, scooters, corner-store doors, and voices carried farther than anyone wants in warm weather.
Lena's apartment smells of hot dust, coffee, old headphone foam, and occasionally wine cartons when she brings shop leftovers home.
Farah's shop smells of cork dust, citrus peel, cardboard, and the damp mineral note of spilled white wine on concrete.
The Wildwood trailer carries salt, mildew, old sunscreen, and the stubborn synthetic heat of fiberglass and vinyl.
Rainy fall chapters should remember slick brick, wet denim, late buses, and the way water makes the city sound both closer and more muffled.
